upper_intermediate.title: Gerund or Infinitive: Verb Patterns
upper_intermediate.content:
📌 Động từ THEO SAU bởi Gerund (-ing)
Một số động từ LUÔN luôn đi với Gerund:

Thói quen & Kỹ năng: enjoy, finish, practice, consider, imagine, appreciate, involve, miss, risk, keep, can't help, can't stand

Thừa nhận & Phủ nhận: admit, deny, avoid, suggest, recommend, recommend

Ví dụ:
• She enjoys reading books in her free time.
• He finished doing his homework before dinner.
• They admitted making a mistake in the report.
• She suggested meeting at the café tomorrow.
• I can't help laughing when I see him.
📌 Động từ THEO SAU bởi To-Infinitive
Một số động từ LUÔN luôn đi với To-Infinitive:

Quyết định & Kế hoạch: decide, agree, refuse, promise, hope, expect, plan, arrange, prepare, threaten, volunteer

Khả năng & Kỹ năng: manage, fail, afford, learn, tend, aim, pretend, seem, appear

Nguyện vọng: want, would like, would love, would prefer, would hate, wish, desire

Ví dụ:
• She decided to move to a new city.
• He agreed to help us with the project.
• They refused to accept the offer.
• I hope to see you soon at the conference.
• She learned to play the piano at age seven.
📌 Động từ đổi NGHĨA khi dùng Gerund và Infinitive
Động từGerund (-ing)Infinitive (to + V)
rememberNhớ lại ký ức quá khứNhớ làm việc gì (trước nhớ, sau làm)
forgetKý ức không bao giờ quên (thường phủ định)Quên không làm việc gì
tryThử làm thí nghiệmCố gắng làm việc khó
stopNgừng hẳn một hoạt độngDừng lại để làm việc khác
needCần được làm (bị động)Cần phải làm gì đó
like/loveThói quen chungDịp cụ thể một lần
preferThói quen chungDịp cụ thể
📌 Động từ + Object + To-Infinitive
ask, tell, want, would like, expect, need, encourage, advise, allow, permit, forbid, remind, warn, persuade, invite, order, force, teach, hire

Ví dụ:
• I asked her to come with us to the party.
• She told me to wait here until she returned.
• They encouraged us to try the new restaurant.
• He reminded me to submit the report before Friday.
• The doctor advised him to rest for a week.
📌 Động từ + Bare Infinitive (không có to)
let, make, help

Ví dụ:
• She let me use her laptop for the presentation.
• They made him repeat the entire answer.
• Can you help me carry these boxes? (= help me to carry)
I remember meeting her at the conference. She wore a red dress. Nhớ lại sự kiện quá khứ → Gerund
Please remember to lock the door when you leave. Nhớ làm việc cần làm → Infinitive
Try using a different method. Maybe it will work better. Thử nghiệm phương pháp → Gerund
I tried to open the window, but it was stuck. Cố gắng làm việc khó → Infinitive

exercises.q: She suggested ___ a different approach to solve the problem.
I can't imagine ___ such a delicious meal by myself.
He decided ___ the university application before the deadline.
They denied ___ anything wrong during the investigation.
She enjoys ___ in the park early in the morning.
I remember ___ him at the party last year. He was very funny.
exercises.exp: remember + doing = nhớ lại ký ức

exercises.q: Please remember ___ the lights when you leave the room.
exercises.opts: to turn off,turning off,turn off,turned off
exercises.exp: remember + to do = nhớ làm việc cần làm

exercises.q: He admitted ___ the money from the company account.
I would love ___ to your wedding ceremony next month.
exercises.exp: would love + to do = mong muốn

exercises.q: She recommended ___ the new Italian restaurant downtown.
He risks ___ his career by speaking publicly against the policy.
They refused ___ the terms of the contract without negotiation.
I can't help ___ when I see his photos from that trip.
exercises.exp: can't help + doing = không thể không làm

exercises.q: She hopes ___ the promotion by the end of this quarter.
He kept ___ despite all the obstacles in his way.
exercises.exp: keep + doing = tiếp tục cố gắng

exercises.q: I look forward to ___ you at the conference next week.
exercises.exp: look forward to + doing (to là giới từ)

exercises.q: She avoided ___ him about the surprise party until it was time.
They agreed ___ the meeting to the following Monday.
I'll never forget ___ my first job interview. I was so nervous.
exercises.exp: never forget + doing = ký ức không bao giờ quên

exercises.q: He offered ___ us with the moving process this weekend.
I appreciate you ___ the time to meet with me today.
She didn't expect ___ the exam since she didn't study.
He pretended not ___ the message on his phone.
They finished ___ the project ahead of the scheduled deadline.
I would like ___ a refund for the defective product I purchased.
She dreams of ___ a famous writer and publishing several novels.
exercises.exp: dream of + doing (of là giới từ)

exercises.q: He tends to ___ early when he has important meetings.
exercises.opts: waking up,to wake up,wake up,woke up
exercises.exp: tend + to do = có xu hướng

exercises.q: The teacher asked the students ___ their assignments before Friday.
I don't mind ___ overtime if the project requires it.
They encouraged her ___ for the position despite her doubts.
She suggested ___ (change) the meeting time to avoid conflicts.
I can't imagine ___ (travel) to space one day. It seems so dangerous.
exercises.exp: can't imagine + doing = không thể tưởng tượng

exercises.q: He decided ___ (leave) the company and start his own business.
They denied ___ (steal) the confidential documents from the server.
She enjoys ___ (read) novels before going to bed every night.
I remember ___ (see) the Northern Lights for the first time. It was breathtaking.
exercises.exp: remember + doing = nhớ lại ký ức

exercises.q: Please remember ___ (turn) off all electronic devices before the flight.
exercises.exp: remember + to do = nhớ làm việc cần làm

exercises.q: He admitted ___ (make) a serious mistake in the financial report.
I would love ___ (visit) the ancient ruins in Egypt someday.
exercises.exp: would love + to do = mong muốn

exercises.q: She recommended ___ (try) the spicy curry at that new restaurant.
He risks ___ (lose) his reputation by associating with controversial people.
They refused ___ (sign) the agreement without reviewing all terms carefully.
I can't help ___ (feel) emotional whenever I watch that movie.
exercises.exp: can't help + doing = không thể không

exercises.q: She hopes ___ (find) a solution to the environmental problem soon.
He kept ___ (practice) the piano even after midnight to prepare for the concert.
I look forward to ___ (hear) the good news about my application.
exercises.exp: look forward to + doing (to là giới từ)

exercises.q: She avoided ___ (mention) the topic until she was ready to discuss it.
They agreed ___ (extend) the deadline for the project submission.
I'll never forget ___ (receive) the acceptance letter from my dream university.
exercises.exp: never forget + doing = ký ức không bao giờ quên

exercises.q: He offered ___ (drive) us to the airport early tomorrow morning.
I appreciate you ___ (help) me with the research for my thesis.
She didn't expect ___ (win) the award given the strong competition.
He pretended not ___ (hear) the rumor about his transfer to another department.
They finished ___ (review) all applications by the end of the week.
I would like ___ (express) my sincere gratitude for your support.
She dreams of ___ (become) a successful entrepreneur in the tech industry.
exercises.exp: dream of + doing (of là giới từ)

exercises.q: He tends to ___ (forget) important dates when he's under stress.
exercises.exp: tend + to do = có xu hướng

exercises.q: The manager asked the team ___ (prepare) a detailed presentation for the client.
I don't mind ___ (wake) up early if it means avoiding traffic.
They encouraged the students ___ (participate) in the science fair competition.

reorder.words: she suggested changing the marketing strategy to attract younger customers
I can't imagine living without access to the internet in today's world
he decided to pursue a career in medicine despite his love for art
they denied knowing anything about the missing documents from the archive
she enjoys reading mystery novels during her leisure time on weekends
I remember meeting the CEO at the conference five years ago in London
please remember to lock all doors and windows before leaving for vacation
he admitted making false statements during the police interview yesterday
I would love to travel around Southeast Asia after I finish my studies
she recommended trying the vegetarian options at the new restaurant downtown
he risks losing his driver's license if he continues speeding on highways
they refused to negotiate the price after the initial offer was made
I can't help feeling anxious when I have to speak in front of large audiences
she hopes to complete her PhD research within the next two years
he kept asking questions despite the professor's explanation about the topic
I look forward to hearing the final results of the competition next month
she avoided talking about her childhood trauma until she started therapy
they agreed to extend the trial period for the new software by three months
I'll never forget graduating from university and receiving my diploma
he offered to help his neighbours move their furniture to the new house
I appreciate you taking time out of your busy schedule to mentor me
she didn't expect to receive such positive feedback on her first book
he pretended not to notice the mistake in the contract until later
they finished decorating the venue just before the guests started arriving
I would like to thank everyone who supported me during this difficult time
she dreams of becoming a renowned chef with her own television show
he tends to oversleep when he stays up late working on his projects
the teacher asked the students to submit their homework by Friday afternoon
I don't mind waiting for my appointment if the service is worth it
they encouraged their daughter to pursue her passion for music education

passage.text: Last year, my friend decided {{1}} (start) his own technology company. He had always dreamed of {{2}} (become) an entrepreneur since he was young. He refused {{3}} (accept) the traditional career path and wanted {{4}} (create) something innovative. He managed {{5}} (secure) funding from investors after months of pitching his idea. I remember {{6}} (attend) his first product launch with excitement. He kept {{7}} (improve) the product based on customer feedback. He eventually succeeded and now enjoys {{8}} (lead) a team of talented developers. He looks forward to {{9}} (expand) his business internationally next year.

When I was in high school, I avoided {{1}} (speak) in public because I was extremely shy. My teachers recommended {{2}} (join) the debate club to build my confidence. I remember {{3}} (feel) terrified during my first practice session. However, I decided {{4}} (overcome) my fear step by step. My coach suggested {{5}} (prepare) my arguments thoroughly before each debate. I kept {{6}} (practice) and eventually stopped {{7}} (worry) about what others thought of me. Now I don't mind {{8}} (present) in front of large audiences. I even enjoy {{9}} (share) my ideas with people from different backgrounds.

My grandmother has always enjoyed {{1}} (cook) traditional dishes for our family. She taught me how {{2}} (make) my favourite soup when I was young. She suggested {{3}} (add) more vegetables to improve the nutritional value. She keeps {{4}} (experiment) with new recipes while still preserving the original flavors. She refused {{5}} (compromise) on the authentic taste even when people suggested {{6}} (use) modern cooking techniques. I appreciate her {{7}} (dedicate) so much time to preserving our cultural heritage through food. I hope {{8}} (learn) more family recipes from her before she gets too old.

She decided {{1}} (apply) for the scholarship to study abroad after graduation. She hoped {{2}} (receive) the funding by spring. Her professors encouraged her {{3}} (submit) a strong application before the deadline. She spent weeks {{4}} (write) her personal statement and {{5}} (gather) recommendation letters. She finally managed {{6}} (complete) everything on time despite her busy schedule. She looks forward to {{7}} (study) at one of the top universities in Europe. I remember {{8}} (congratulate) her when she received the acceptance letter. She admitted {{9}} (feel) overwhelmed but excited about this new chapter in her life.

Last summer, our family planned {{1}} (visit) several European countries during our vacation. We agreed {{2}} (split) the costs equally among all members. My parents suggested {{3}} (book) accommodations in advance to avoid last-minute problems. I enjoyed {{4}} (explore) historical sites and {{5}} (take) photos at famous landmarks. My brother tried {{6}} (learn) basic phrases in each local language we visited. He kept {{7}} (practice) speaking with native speakers even when he made mistakes. We stopped {{8}} (buy) souvenirs for relatives back home. I look forward to {{9}} (return) someday and explore countries we missed.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một quyết định quan trọng trong cuộc sống và những người đã ảnh hưởng đến quyết định đó
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Decide/agree/refuse + to do; encourage/advise/warn + to do; suggest/recommend + doing; remember/forget + doing
writing_prompts.example: Three years ago, I made a crucial decision to change my career path. My mentor encouraged me to try something new instead of staying in a job I didn't enjoy. She advised me to consider my true passions and skills before making a final choice. My family supported my decision and agreed to help me financially during the transition period. My best friend suggested taking professional development courses to prepare for the new field. I remember feeling nervous about the change, but I refused to let fear stop me from growing. I kept improving my skills and eventually managed to succeed in my new career. Now I look forward to achieving even greater things in the future.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một thói quen hoặc hoạt động bạn yêu thích và lý do tại sao nó quan trọng với bạn
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Enjoy/like/love + -ing; can't imagine + -ing; keep + doing; look forward to + -ing; appreciate + doing
writing_prompts.example: I have always enjoyed reading books since I was a child. Every evening before bed, I spend at least an hour with a good novel. I can't imagine living without access to books because they have shaped my worldview significantly. My parents encouraged me to keep reading even when I was busy with schoolwork. I appreciate having such a peaceful hobby that allows me to escape from daily stress. I look forward to visiting the new library in our neighborhood to discover new authors. Reading has helped me improve my vocabulary and writing skills considerably. My friends often ask me for book recommendations, and I enjoy sharing my favorite titles with them.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một trải nghiệm học tập đáng nhớ và những bài học bạn rút ra từ đó
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Learn + to do; teach + to do; manage + to do; fail + to do; remember + doing; admit + doing; suggest + doing
writing_prompts.example: When I was learning English, I remember struggling with speaking in front of others. My teacher suggested practicing with native speakers to build confidence. I admit feeling embarrassed when I made mistakes during conversations. However, I refused to give up and kept practicing every day. I learned to speak more fluently after months of dedicated effort. My parents encouraged me to keep going and promised to support my journey. I managed to pass the English proficiency exam with a high score. Now I look forward to using my language skills for international business opportunities. I appreciate everyone who helped me along the way.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một người bạn hoặc người thân đã có ảnh hưởng tích cực đến cuộc sống của bạn
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Encourage/advise/warn + to do; remind + to do; tell + to do; help + to do; promise + to do; hope + to do
hint_words: warned me not to give up
writing_prompts.example: My grandmother has always been my biggest supporter and role model. She encouraged me to pursue my education despite financial difficulties in our family. She advised me to be patient and persistent when facing challenges in life. My grandmother promised to help me in any way she could, even when she was struggling herself. She reminded me to stay humble and grateful for every small achievement. I remember her telling me to believe in my abilities and never compare myself to others. She refused to let me quit when things got difficult and always found ways to motivate me. I hope to become a source of strength for others just as she has been for me.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một kế hoạch hoặc dự định trong tương lai và những gì bạn đang làm để chuẩn bị
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Plan/hope/expect + to do; arrange + to do; decide + to do; look forward + to doing; keep + doing; manage + to do
writing_prompts.example: I have been planning to pursue a master's degree abroad for the past two years. I hope to specialize in environmental science at a university with excellent research facilities. I have been saving money and preparing my application documents since last spring. My professor agreed to write a strong recommendation letter for my application. I arranged to take the required English proficiency test next month. I look forward to starting this new academic journey and meeting scholars from around the world. My family promised to support me financially during my studies overseas. I keep reminding myself to stay focused on my goals and not get distracted by temporary setbacks.

upper_intermediate.title: Would rather, Would prefer: Expressing Preference
upper_intermediate.content:
📌 Would rather / Would sooner + Infinitive
Would rather + infinitive (không có to)

Sử dụng để diễn đạt sở thích trong tình huống cụ thể.

Cấu trúc:
• I'd rather have tea, please.
• She'd rather stay at home tonight.
• They'd rather go by train than drive.

So sánh với would sooner:
I'd sooner stay here than go out in this rain.
📌 Would rather + Subject + Past Simple
Dùng để nói về mong muốn về hiện tại hoặc tương lai

Cấu trúc:
• We'd rather she was here with us now.
• I'd rather you came earlier tomorrow.
• She'd rather I picked her up after the meeting.
• Would you rather we went by bus or by train?

Chú ý: Có thể dùng were thay cho was sau would rather + subject với I/he/she.

Ví dụ:
• I'd rather he were here now. (= I wish he were here)
• She'd rather I wasn't so nervous about the interview.
📌 Prefer / Would prefer + To-Infinitive
Dùng để nói về sở thích cho dịp cụ thể

Cấu trúc:
• I would prefer to stay in a hotel near the airport.
• Most clients prefer to have breakfast in their room.
• I'd prefer to leave early rather than be late.

So sánh với would rather:
I would rather stay here. = I would prefer to stay here.

Rather than + infinitive (không có to):
• I would prefer to go early rather than stay late.
• She prefers to walk rather than drive.
📌 Prefer + -ing (Sở thích chung)
Dùng để nói về sở thích chung, thói quen

Cấu trúc:
• I love running, but he prefers cycling.
• He prefers walking to cycling for daily commute.

Prefer + noun/gerund + to + noun/gerund:
• She prefers tea to coffee.
• He prefers walking to taking the bus.

Chú ý: To sau prefer trong cấu trúc này là giới từ, nên động từ phải dùng dạng -ing.
I'd rather stay at home tonight than go to that crowded party.Would rather + V (không to) + than + V
She'd rather you came tomorrow instead of today.Would rather + subject + past simple
I prefer travelling by train to flying whenever possible.Prefer + -ing + to + -ing (sở thích chung)

exercises.q: I'd rather ___ at home tonight instead of going to the cinema.
She'd rather I ___ to the meeting rather than call in.
I prefer brown sugar ___ white sugar.
We'd rather ___ by train than fly if the journey is short.
I'd prefer ___ the project today rather than leave it for tomorrow.
He prefers working ___ at the office for better concentration.
Would you rather we ___ the appointment to next week?
I'd sooner ___ at a beach resort than spend time in a busy city.
She prefers coffee ___ tea in the morning.
I would rather ___ to classical music while working.
He'd rather his wife ___ at home while he's away on business.
I prefer ___ my own car when traveling to unfamiliar places.
They would prefer ___ the meeting in the morning rather than afternoon.
She prefers reading ___ watching television in her free time.
I'd rather ___ by credit card than cash for online purchases.
Would you rather we ___ the discussion after lunch instead of now?
He prefers cooking at home ___ eating out at restaurants.
I would rather ___ my thesis next semester than rush it this term.
She'd rather I ___ about this sensitive topic until she's ready.
exercises.exp: would rather + subject + past simple (phủ định)

exercises.q: My mother prefers traditional medicine ___ modern treatments for minor illnesses.
We'd rather ___ the outdoor event if it rains heavily.
I prefer ___ by window seat when I'm on a long flight.
He'd rather his daughter ___ at a university close to home.
They prefer tea ___ coffee in the afternoon.
I'd rather ___ the conference call than attend in person.
She prefers writing emails ___ making phone calls for work.
Would you rather we ___ at a restaurant or cook at home tonight?
I would prefer ___ early to avoid the rush hour traffic.
He prefers vegetables ___ meat in his daily diet.
I'd rather ___ (stay) at home tonight than go to the crowded concert.
She'd rather I ___ (come) to the office tomorrow instead of today.
I prefer tea ___ coffee in the morning. It helps me wake up gently.
We'd rather ___ (travel) by train than fly to reduce our carbon footprint.
I'd prefer ___ (work) from home tomorrow rather than commute to the office.
He prefers reading books ___ watching television during his free time.
Would you rather we ___ (reschedule) the meeting for next week instead?
I'd sooner ___ (eat) at a local restaurant than a chain restaurant when traveling.
She prefers classical music ___ pop music for relaxation.
I would rather ___ (listen) to jazz while reading than complete silence.
He'd rather his wife ___ (work) part-time instead of full-time after the baby is born.
I prefer ___ (take) public transportation to reduce traffic congestion.
They would prefer ___ (meet) at their office rather than at a neutral location.
She prefers hiking in the mountains ___ swimming in the ocean.
I'd rather ___ (pay) by card than carry large amounts of cash.
Would you rather we ___ (go) to the Italian restaurant or the Japanese one?
He prefers writing letters ___ sending text messages to maintain personal connections.
I would rather ___ (submit) the assignment today than ask for an extension.
She'd rather I ___ (not/mention) her salary during the team meeting.
exercises.exp: would rather + subject + past simple (phủ định)

exercises.q: My father prefers gardening ___ playing sports in his retirement.
We'd rather ___ (watch) the match at home than go to the stadium tonight.
I prefer ___ (study) in the morning when my mind is fresh and alert.
He'd rather his son ___ (become) a teacher than a lawyer.
They prefer homemade food ___ restaurant meals for health reasons.
I'd rather ___ (walk) to work than drive when the weather is nice.
She prefers summer ___ winter because she loves warm weather and beach activities.
Would you rather we ___ (discuss) this matter now or wait until tomorrow?
I would prefer ___ (arrive) early to the airport to avoid any last-minute rush.
He prefers living in the countryside ___ in a big city for peace and quiet.
I'd rather ___ (have) a quiet dinner at home than go to a noisy party.

reorder.words: I'd rather stay at home tonight than go to the crowded cinema
she'd rather I came to the party tomorrow instead of today
I prefer tea to coffee especially in the early morning
we'd rather travel by train than fly for short distances
I'd prefer to start the project today rather than leave it for tomorrow
he prefers working from home to working in the office every day
would you rather we postponed the meeting to next week
I'd sooner relax at a beach resort than spend time in a busy city
she prefers reading books to watching television in her leisure time
I would rather listen to classical music while studying than complete silence
he'd rather his daughter studied at a university close to home
I prefer to take my own car when traveling to unfamiliar destinations
they would prefer to meet at their office rather than at a coffee shop
she prefers hiking in the mountains to swimming in the ocean
I'd rather pay by card than carry large amounts of cash
would you rather we went to the Italian restaurant or the Japanese one
he prefers writing emails to making phone calls for business communication
I would rather submit the report today than ask for an extension
she'd rather I didn't mention her personal plans during the meeting
my father prefers gardening to playing sports in his free time
we'd rather watch the movie at home than go to the cinema tonight
I prefer to study in the morning when my mind is fresh and alert
he'd rather his son became a doctor than a businessman in the future
they prefer homemade food to restaurant meals for health reasons
I'd rather walk to work than drive when the weather is pleasant
she prefers summer to winter because she loves warm weather activities
would you rather we discussed the budget today or tomorrow morning
I would prefer to arrive at the airport early to avoid any unexpected delays
he prefers living in the countryside to living in a big metropolitan city
I'd rather have a quiet dinner at home than attend a loud social gathering

passage.text: Planning a vacation can be challenging when family members have different preferences. My husband prefers {{1}} (go) to the mountains for hiking while I would rather {{2}} (relax) at a sunny beach. We both agree that we'd rather {{3}} (stay) at a hotel than rent an apartment because we prefer {{4}} (have) room service available. My husband prefers {{5}} (travel) by car so he can stop wherever he wants, but I would rather {{6}} (fly) to save time. We both prefer {{7}} (explore) local restaurants rather than cook our own meals. I prefer {{8}} (visit) historical sites while he prefers {{9}} (go) on adventurous activities. In the end, we agreed to spend the first week at a beach and the second week hiking in the mountains.

Choosing a career path requires careful consideration of personal preferences. My brother prefers {{1}} (work) in a corporate environment while I would rather {{2}} (run) my own business. He prefers {{3}} (have) a stable salary and regular working hours, but I prefer {{4}} (take) risks for potential greater rewards. My parents prefer {{5}} (see) us work in prestigious companies rather than start something uncertain. However, they understand that we'd rather {{6}} (pursue) our passions than follow traditional paths. I prefer {{7}} (be) my own boss while my brother prefers {{8}} (collaborate) with colleagues in a team. We both agree that we'd rather {{9}} (do) something meaningful than just earn money.

Lifestyle choices often reflect our personal values and preferences. I prefer {{1}} (wake) up early in the morning to exercise before work while my roommate would rather {{2}} (sleep) until noon on weekends. I prefer {{3}} (eat) healthy homemade food to eating fast food, but my roommate prefers {{4}} (order) delivery from restaurants. She'd rather {{5}} (spend) money on experiences than save for the future. I would rather {{6}} (save) money for emergencies while she prefers {{7}} (travel) whenever possible. We both prefer {{8}} (live) in the city center rather than in the suburbs because we prefer {{9}} (have) easy access to public transportation. My roommate prefers {{10}} (hang) out with friends at night while I prefer staying home with a good book.

Educational choices can shape our future significantly. My sister prefers {{1}} (study) medicine because she prefers {{2}} (help) others rather than work in a business setting. She would rather {{3}} (work) in a hospital than run her own clinic. My brother prefers {{4}} (pursue) a career in technology because he prefers {{5}} (solve) complex problems rather than deal with patients. Our parents prefer {{6}} (see) us work in stable professions but understand we'd rather {{7}} (follow) our own paths. I prefer {{8}} (become) a teacher because I prefer {{9}} (work) with students rather than in a corporate environment. We'd all rather {{10}} (make) a positive impact on society than earn high salaries.

Making everyday decisions often involves comparing options and expressing preferences. In the morning, I prefer {{1}} (have) coffee rather than tea to start my day. My colleague would rather {{2}} (walk) to the office than take the bus because she prefers {{3}} (exercise) during commute. I prefer {{4}} (work) in silence while she prefers {{5}} (listen) to music while working. At lunch, she prefers {{6}} (eat) salad while I prefer a hearty meal. We both prefer {{7}} (spend) our lunch break together rather than eat alone. She would rather {{8}} (go) to the new Vietnamese restaurant than the Italian one. I prefer {{9}} (try) different cuisines when we go out. We both prefer {{10}} (save) money by bringing lunch from home at least twice a week.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả sở thích của bạn về nơi ở và lý do tại sao bạn thích nó hơn những lựa chọn khác
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Would rather + infinitive; prefer + to infinitive; prefer + noun/gerund + to + noun/gerund; would rather + subject + past simple
writing_prompts.example: I would rather live in a small coastal town than in a big metropolitan city because I prefer a peaceful lifestyle to the hectic pace of urban areas. Every morning, I prefer to wake up to the sound of waves rather than car horns and construction noise. My friends would rather stay in the city where all the entertainment options are available, but I prefer the simple pleasures of watching sunsets from my balcony. I would rather spend my evenings walking along the beach than attending crowded social events in the city center. My parents would rather see me living closer to them in the city, but they understand that I prefer following my own heart and choosing the environment where I feel most content and happy.

writing_prompts.vi: So sánh hai sở thích trong cuộc sống và giải thích tại sao bạn thích cái này hơn cái kia
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Prefer + noun/gerund + to + noun/gerund; would rather + V (than + V); would prefer + to infinitive (rather than + V); would sooner + V
hint_words: I'd rather cook than eat out
I prefer to travel by train rather than fly
She'd rather stay home than go out
He prefers working early rather than late
We'd rather eat homemade food than restaurant food
I prefer the quiet to the noise
writing_prompts.example: I prefer reading books to watching television because I believe reading allows me to use my imagination more actively than watching does. When I read, I can picture the characters and settings in my own unique way, whereas when I watch a movie, the images are already provided for me. I'd rather spend my evenings with a good novel than sit in front of a screen for hours. I prefer to read non-fiction books to learn new ideas rather than read fiction for entertainment alone. My sister prefers watching movies to reading books because she finds reading too time-consuming, but I would rather take my time exploring detailed stories than rush through quick visual experiences. We both agree that we prefer activities that help us relax after a long workday.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một quyết định hàng ngày liên quan đến sở thích và giải thích lựa chọn của bạn
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Would rather + infinitive; would rather + subject + past simple; prefer + to infinitive; prefer + gerund + to + gerund
hint_words: I would rather wake up early
I prefer to work at night
We'd rather eat at home than go out
I'd rather not talk about it
They prefer staying in to going out
I prefer making my own lunch
writing_prompts.example: Every morning, I would rather wake up early to exercise before work than sleep in and skip my workout routine. I prefer to start my day with physical activity because it gives me energy and improves my mood throughout the day. My roommate would rather sleep until the last minute and skip breakfast, but I prefer to have a proper meal in the morning. At work, I would rather complete my most difficult tasks first while my colleagues prefer to handle easy tasks first. I prefer to work in a quiet environment rather than an open office where people constantly interrupt each other. My boss would rather have us work in teams than individually, but I think the project would succeed better if we had more independence. Overall, I believe making choices based on our preferences helps us lead more satisfying lives.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả sở thích của bạn về phương tiện di chuyển và tại sao bạn thích chúng hơn các lựa chọn khác
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Prefer + to + infinitive; would rather + V; prefer + gerund + to + gerund; would prefer + to infinitive; rather than + V
hint_words: I prefer to travel by train rather than fly
I'd rather drive than use public transport
She prefers walking to taking the bus
We'd rather cycle to work than drive
He prefers taking the train to flying
I would rather walk than take a taxi
They prefer traveling slowly to rushing
I'd rather commute by subway than drive
writing_prompts.example: When it comes to transportation, I would rather travel by train than fly because I prefer having more space to relax during long journeys. I enjoy watching the scenery pass by the window rather than being confined to a small airplane seat for hours. My friends would rather fly to save time, but I prefer to use that extra time for reading or working on my laptop while on the train. I prefer traveling by train to driving because I don't have to deal with traffic jams or find parking spaces. I would rather arrive at my destination feeling refreshed than exhausted from sitting in a cramped car for hours. For short distances, I prefer walking to taking the bus because I enjoy the fresh air and the ability to stop whenever I want to take photos of interesting things I see along the way.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về sở thích của bạn trong việc lựa chọn thực phẩm và ẩm thực
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Prefer + noun + to + noun; prefer + gerund + to + gerund; would rather + V (than + V); would prefer + to infinitive (rather than + V)
hint_words: I'd rather cook at home than eat out
She prefers homemade food to restaurant meals
We'd rather drink water than soda
He prefers spicy food to bland food
I would rather have tea than coffee
I'd rather eat early than late at night
writing_prompts.example: Regarding food preferences, I would rather cook at home than eat at restaurants because I prefer knowing exactly what ingredients go into my meals. I prefer homemade food to processed food because I believe it is healthier and more nutritious. My family would rather eat traditional Vietnamese dishes than international cuisines because we prefer our cultural food heritage. I prefer vegetables to meat and try to include them in every meal for better health. I would rather drink herbal tea than coffee in the evening because I prefer the calming effect of tea to the stimulating effect of caffeine. My roommate prefers ordering food delivery rather than cooking, but I think she misses out on the joy of preparing meals yourself. Overall, I believe that our food preferences shape not only our health but also our cultural identity.

upper_intermediate.title: Reporting Verbs: Admit doing, refuse to do, etc.
upper_intermediate.content:
📌 Nhóm 1: Động từ + Gerund (-ing)
Các động từ: admit, avoid, deny, finish, keep, miss, practice, suggest, recommend, consider, enjoy, imagine, appreciate, risk, involve

Ví dụ:
• He admitted stealing the money from the cash register.
• She avoids talking about her personal problems at work.
• They denied taking any bribes during the investigation.
• He finished eating his dinner and went to bed immediately.
• She kept complaining about the poor service for hours.
• They suggested meeting at the café to discuss the project further.
• I enjoy reading books in my free time every weekend.
• She risked losing her job by speaking up against the manager's decision.
• The job involves working overtime on weekends occasionally.
📌 Nhóm 2: Động từ + To-Infinitive
Các động từ: agree, decide, hope, expect, refuse, promise, offer, seem, appear, pretend, want, would like, would love, would prefer, would hate, manage, fail, afford, plan, arrange, prepare, attempt, threaten, volunteer, ask

Ví dụ:
• She agreed to help us with the move this Saturday.
• They decided to postpone the meeting until next week.
• I hope to see you at the graduation ceremony next month.
• He refused to answer any more questions from the journalist.
• She offered to drive us to the train station early in the morning.
• He promised to call me back as soon as he arrived at the office.
• I expect to receive the results by the end of this week.
• She managed to pass the exam despite her limited preparation.
• They failed to reach an agreement during the negotiations.
📌 Nhóm 3: Động từ + Object + To-Infinitive
Các động từ: ask, tell, want, would like, expect, need, encourage, advise, warn, remind, persuade, invite, order, force, permit, forbid, hire

Ví dụ:
• I asked her to come with us to the concert on Friday night.
• She told me to wait here until she finished her work.
• They encouraged us to try the new restaurant downtown.
• He reminded me to submit the report before the Friday deadline.
• My doctor advised me to rest for at least a week.
• She warned him not to be late for the important meeting.
• The teacher asked the students to prepare for the exam thoroughly.
• They invited us to attend the wedding ceremony next Sunday.
📌 Nhóm 4: Động từ + Preposition + Gerund
insist on, think of, dream of, approve of, look forward to, object to, feel like, count on, rely on, consist of

Ví dụ:
• My mother insisted on lending me money for the trip.
• I'm thinking of moving to a bigger apartment next year.
• She dreams of becoming a famous writer when she grows up.
• He doesn't approve of spending money on luxury items.
• I look forward to meeting you at the conference next week.
• She objected to paying such a high price for the service.
• I feel like going out for dinner tonight. How about you?
• I'm counting on you helping me with this difficult task.
📌 Nhóm 5: Động từ + Bare Infinitive (không có to)
let, make, help

Ví dụ:
• She let me use her laptop for my presentation.
• They made him repeat the entire answer because it was unclear.
• Can you help me carry these heavy boxes to the storage room?
• My father helped me understand the complex mathematical concepts.
He admitted stealing the money from the company. Nhóm 1: admit + doing
She refused to answer any more questions about the incident. Nhóm 2: refuse + to do
I asked her to come with us to the party. Nhóm 3: ask + object + to do
I'm looking forward to seeing you soon at the reunion. Nhóm 4: look forward to + doing

exercises.q: He admitted ___ the money from the company's account.
exercises.exp: admit + doing = thừa nhận đã làm gì

exercises.q: She refused ___ any further questions from the police.
I asked him ___ his presentation before the meeting started.
They suggested ___ the project deadline to next month.
She offered ___ us with the research work.
exercises.exp: offer + to do = đề nghị giúp

exercises.q: He denied ___ anything wrong during the experiment.
My teacher advised me ___ for the scholarship application.
I look forward to ___ from you about the job opportunity.
She encouraged her son ___ harder for the upcoming exams.
They decided ___ the company to expand to international markets.
exercises.opts: for the company to expand,that the company expands,the company expanding,expand

exercises.q: He kept ___ about his problems even though no one asked.
The doctor suggested he ___ more water and rest adequately.
She told her assistant ___ the documents ready before the meeting.
I expect ___ the promotion by the end of this quarter.
They threatened ___ legal action if the payment was not made.
She risked ___ her career by reporting the corruption.
He warned us ___ the unstable weather conditions during our trip.
I would like ___ your thoughts on the proposed plan.
She admitted ___ the answer to the test when she was caught.
They arranged ___ the conference for the following Tuesday.
He pretended not ___ the situation but we all knew the truth.
The manager asked the team ___ their ideas during the brainstorming session.
She insisted on ___ the original plan despite everyone's concerns.
I can't imagine ___ such a difficult situation without any help.
They refused ___ the terms of the agreement without further negotiation.
He promised ___ me with the move next Saturday morning.
She enjoys ___ in her garden early in the morning before work.
The company agreed ___ the contract with the new supplier.
I reminded him ___ the meeting minutes to all team members.
She dreamed of ___ a successful writer and publishing several books.
He admitted ___ (steal) the confidential documents from the archive.
She refused ___ (answer) any more questions about the controversial topic.
I asked her ___ (prepare) a detailed report before the quarterly meeting.
They suggested ___ (postpone) the wedding ceremony until next spring.
He offered ___ (drive) us to the airport at five in the morning.
She denied ___ (take) the credit card that was found in her bag.
The professor advised the students ___ (submit) their assignments on time.
I look forward to ___ (meet) you at the international conference next month.
My parents encouraged me ___ (pursue) a career in medical research.
They decided ___ (start) their own business despite the risks involved.
He kept ___ (complain) about the traffic even though we couldn't change it.
The doctor suggested he ___ (reduce) his working hours to avoid burnout.
She told her colleague ___ (double-check) all the calculations before submission.
I expect ___ (receive) the confirmation email by the end of today.
They threatened ___ (expose) the scandal if the CEO did not resign.
She risked ___ (lose) her reputation by speaking publicly against the policy.
He warned us ___ (be) careful about the potential financial risks in the investment.
I would like ___ (express) my sincere gratitude for your support during this difficult time.
She admitted ___ (cheat) on the exam when she was confronted with evidence.
They arranged ___ (meet) with the client at their office next Monday morning.
He pretended not ___ (understand) the instructions but everyone saw through his act.
The manager asked the team ___ (work) overtime to meet the tight deadline.
She insisted on ___ (use) only organic ingredients in her restaurant.
I can't imagine ___ (work) in such a stressful environment for a long time.
They refused ___ (sign) the contract without reviewing all the terms carefully.
He promised ___ (call) me as soon as he arrived at the destination.
She enjoys ___ (read) historical novels during her leisure time on weekends.
The company agreed ___ (extend) the warranty period for all customers.
I reminded my friend ___ (bring) the necessary documents to the appointment.
She dreamed of ___ (become) a renowned scientist and making important discoveries.

reorder.words: he admitted stealing the confidential files from the company database
she refused to answer any more questions about the ongoing investigation
I asked her to prepare a detailed presentation before the board meeting
they suggested postponing the event until the weather conditions improve
he offered to help his elderly neighbour carry groceries every week
she denied taking the money even when the CCTV footage was shown
the doctor advised me to rest for at least two weeks after the surgery
I look forward to meeting you at the annual conference next September
my parents encouraged me to pursue a career in law despite my doubts
they decided to launch the new product in the first quarter of next year
he kept asking questions about the project despite the manager's explanation
the teacher suggested reviewing all chapters before the final examination
she told her assistant to double-check all the data before final submission
I expect to receive the confirmation email by the end of business today
they threatened to take legal action if the payment was not received by Friday
she risked losing her promotion by refusing to work overtime on weekends
he warned us to be careful about the unexpected changes in the market conditions
I would like to thank everyone who contributed to the success of this project
she admitted making a mistake in the financial report during the meeting
they arranged to meet the clients at the downtown office for negotiations
he pretended not to see me when we passed each other at the shopping mall
the manager asked the team to submit their proposals before the Friday deadline
she insisted on using the original design despite the client's suggestions for changes
I can't imagine living in such a remote area without modern facilities
they refused to accept the terms of the agreement without further discussion
he promised to support his daughter's education throughout her university years
she enjoys reading books in her free time especially historical fiction novels
the company agreed to extend the trial period for the new software by three months
I reminded my colleague to bring the presentation materials to the meeting
she dreams of becoming a successful entrepreneur in the technology industry

passage.text: At yesterday's meeting, the CEO {{1}} (admit / make) a serious error in the company's financial planning. He {{2}} (refuse / reveal) the exact details but promised {{3}} (correct) the problem immediately. The CFO {{4}} (advise / implement) new controls to prevent similar mistakes in the future. The board {{5}} (ask / investigate) the issue thoroughly before making any official statements. They {{6}} (suggest / postpone) the announcement to shareholders until the investigation is complete. The marketing director {{7}} (offer / help) with the crisis management strategy. The HR manager {{8}} (encourage / communicate) honestly with all employees about the situation. The IT department {{9}} (agree / develop) a new tracking system to monitor financial transactions more effectively.

During the police interrogation, the suspect {{1}} (deny / steal) any valuable items from the scene. He {{2}} (admit / be) present at the location but claimed {{3}} (leave) before the crime occurred. The detective {{4}} (warn / give) any false statements would lead to serious consequences. The lawyer {{5}} (advise / remain) silent until the evidence was thoroughly examined. The suspect {{6}} (refuse / answer) several questions about his whereabouts that night. The prosecutor {{7}} (ask / provide) an alibi for the specific time of the incident. The witness {{8}} (claim / see) someone running from the building at around midnight. The judge {{9}} (order / conduct) a full investigation before making any decisions about the case.

In my recent job interview, the hiring manager {{1}} (ask / describe) my previous work experience in detail. I {{2}} (explain / handle) difficult situations with clients in my previous position. He {{3}} (suggest / consider) taking additional courses to improve my skills. The HR director {{4}} (offer / provide) a comprehensive training program for new employees. She {{5}} (encourage / develop) my leadership abilities through mentorship opportunities. I {{6}} (promise / contribute) my best efforts to the company's success. They {{7}} (agree / extend) the probationary period if more training was needed. The team leader {{8}} (invite / join) the department meeting next week to meet everyone. I {{9}} (look forward / start) working in this dynamic and innovative environment.

At the parent-teacher conference, my son's teacher {{1}} (report / make) significant progress in mathematics. She {{2}} (recommend / practice) more problem-solving exercises at home. My son {{3}} (admit / struggle) with the geometry section but {{4}} (keep / try) to improve. The teacher {{5}} (suggest / enroll) him in an after-school tutoring program. I {{6}} (agree / attend) the program if it helps him understand the concepts better. She {{7}} (advise / set) realistic goals for the upcoming examinations. My son {{8}} (promise / work) harder to achieve better results. I {{9}} (look forward / see) his improvement in the coming months. The school {{10}} (offer / provide) additional resources for students who need extra support.

During the team meeting, our project manager {{1}} (announce / implement) several important changes in the workflow. He {{2}} (ask / prepare) detailed reports for the next phase of the project. The team members {{3}} (agree / collaborate) more closely to meet the tight deadlines. She {{4}} (encourage / share) ideas and feedback during the brainstorming sessions. The senior developer {{5}} (offer / mentor) junior programmers to improve their coding skills. He {{6}} (warn / be) careful about potential technical issues in the new system. They {{7}} (decide / allocate) more resources to testing the software thoroughly. I {{8}} (suggest / use) agile methodology for better project management. Our supervisor {{9}} (approve / adopt) the new approach starting next month. We all {{10}} (look forward / complete) the project successfully on time.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một tình huống trong đó bạn phải báo cáo những gì ai đó đã nói hoặc làm
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Admit/deny + doing; agree/refuse + to do; ask/tell + object + to do; suggest/recommend + doing; promise + to do
writing_prompts.example: Last month, during a crucial team meeting, our project manager admitted making several serious mistakes in the initial planning phase. He denied knowing about the budget constraints that caused the delays. Our director asked him to explain the situation in detail. She told the team to remain calm and focus on finding solutions. I suggested trying alternative approaches to recover the lost time. The manager promised to work overtime to fix the problems. My colleague encouraged everyone to stay positive and support each other. He warned us not to repeat the same errors in the future. Despite the challenges, we agreed to collaborate more closely and completed the project successfully on time.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một cuộc phỏng vấn xin việc và những gì được nói trong đó
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Ask/tell + object + to do; advise/encourage + to do; offer/would like + to do; expect/hope + to do; agree/refuse + to do
writing_prompts.example: During my job interview last week, the human resources manager asked me to describe my previous work experience in detail. She told me to explain how I handled difficult situations with clients in my past position. The department head advised me to improve my public speaking skills for future presentations. He encouraged me to apply for the senior position when it becomes available. The company offered to provide comprehensive training for the first three months. They would like to offer me a competitive salary and benefits package. I expected to learn about the company culture and team dynamics. They hoped to find someone who could contribute innovative ideas to the project. After a thorough discussion, they agreed to hire me starting next month.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một cuộc thảo luận quan trọng trong nhóm và cách mọi người đưa ra quyết định
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Suggest/recommend + doing; agree/refuse + to do; decide + to do; insist on + doing; warn + to do; remind + to do
writing_prompts.example: During our team meeting yesterday, our project leader suggested postponing the product launch until next quarter to ensure quality. He recommended trying a new marketing strategy to reach more customers effectively. The sales manager agreed to extend the promotional campaign for another month. However, the finance director refused to accept any further delays due to budget constraints. We decided to implement the original plan with some modifications. Our supervisor insisted on using the most cost-effective approach to minimize expenses. The technical lead warned us about potential risks in the current timeline. The HR manager reminded everyone to stay focused and work together efficiently. I explained the importance of meeting customer expectations while maintaining high standards.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một tình huống bạn phải thuyết phục ai đó làm điều gì đó
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Persuade/convince + to do; advise/warn + to do; encourage + to do; suggest + doing; offer + to do; remind + to do
writing_prompts.example: Last year, I had to persuade my friend to apply for the international scholarship program. I advised him to save all his documents and write a strong personal statement. I warned him not to miss the application deadline because it only came once a year. His parents encouraged him to pursue this opportunity despite his initial hesitation. I suggested taking up some volunteer work to strengthen his application. He offered to help me with my own projects if I supported him through this process. I reminded him to ask for recommendation letters from his professors. After much convincing, he finally agreed to submit his application. He thanked me for pushing him to take this important step in his career.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một cuộc đối thoại quan trọng với người có ảnh hưởng trong cuộc sống của bạn
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Promise + to do; agree/refuse + to do; warn + to do; encourage + to do; admit + doing; deny + doing; ask/tell + object + to do
writing_prompts.example: When I was struggling with my career decisions, my mentor promised to support me through whatever path I chose. He agreed to help me explore different options and find what truly suited my skills and interests. He refused to give up on me even when I felt discouraged and wanted to quit. He warned me about the challenges I might face in the competitive job market. My mentor encouraged me to dream big and never settle for less than I deserved. He admitted struggling with similar decisions in his own career journey. I asked him to share his experiences and advice. He told me to stay strong and trust my abilities. He insisted on trying different approaches before making final decisions. Thanks to his guidance, I finally found a career path that makes me happy and fulfilled.
